title: Fixing Git Cherry-Picked Commit Hash Validation for Non-ASCII Characters

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate cherry-picked commit hashes in Git. A valid cherry-picked commit hash should not contain any non-ASCII characters. However, the function should also handle edge cases where the input hash is empty or null. If the input hash is invalid, the function should return false; otherwise, it should return true. Note that the function should be case-insensitive.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the cherry-picked commit hash.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the commit hash is valid.
statement.notes: The function should be able to handle a large number of inputs, so it should be efficient.

hints.text: Use the ASCII character set to check for non-ASCII characters.

editorial.title: Fixing Git Cherry-Picked Commit Hash Validation for Non-ASCII Characters
editorial.intuition: The function should check if the input string contains any non-ASCII characters.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use the every() method in JavaScript and the all() function in Python to check if all characters in the string are ASCII.
editorial.betterSolution: You can use a regular expression to match all ASCII characters in the string.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the every() method in JavaScript and the all() function in Python to check if all characters in the string are ASCII, and then check if the string is empty or null.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return false for invalid commit hashes and true for valid commit hashes.
editorial.dryRun: Example usage: solve('abc123') should return true, solve('') should return false, and solve('àbc123') should return false.
commonMistakes: Not checking for empty or null input

solutions.python:
import sys
class Solution:
	def solve(self, commitHash: str) -> bool:
		# Read input from stdin
		input = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
		# Parse input
		commitHash = input
		# Check if commit hash is valid
		return commitHash and commitHash.lower().match('^[a-f0-9]{40}$')
	# Print output to stdout
	print(solve(commitHash))
